title: Synthesis of copper oxide nanoparticles using sodium borohydride as a reducing agent and gelatin as a capping agent
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a9jp5","text":"Copper oxide nanoparticles were synthesized using sodium borohydride as a reducing agent and gelatin as a capping agent according to the method previously described with some slight modifications of method of Liu et al., 2012.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2qbf7","text":"Materials","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"218sd","text":"1.         Copper sulfate pentahydrate","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4a1q6","text":"2.         Gelatin","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f3lmj","text":"3.         Sodium borohydride","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"arem4","text":"4.         Sodium hydroxide","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"asao1","text":"5.         Milli-Q water","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4g86q","text":"Equipment","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dn8mc","text":"1.         Magnetic stirring","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fjbfq","text":"2.         A centrifuge","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"75sts","text":"3.         Sonicator","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1qs3r","text":"4.         An oven","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: 10 mL of 0.2 M copper sulfate (or 5.0 g per solution) and 10 mL of 0.1% gelatin dissolved in water were mixed and heated at 70°C for 30 min under magnetic stirring.
10 mL of 0.4 M sodium borohydride (or 0.15 g per solution) was slowly dropped in about 5–10 min into the solution.
The pH of this mixture was adjusted from approximately 2 to 12 by 6 mL of 1 M sodium hydroxide, then heated at 70°C with magnetic stirring for 30 min.
The obtained copper oxide nanoparticles were washed by centrifugation of the nanoparticle dispersion at 8000×g for 30 min.
The obtained pellet was redispersed in 30 mL milli-Q water and this precipitation/washing procedure was repeated two times.
The precipitate was dispersed in absolute ethanol and dried at 60°C for 8 h.
The particles were transferred into light-protecting containers and stored in a desiccator at roomtemperature until use.
creator: Mathurada Sasarom
